mission.title: Install and Configure KubeRay
mission.description: KubeRay is an open-source Kubernetes operator that simplifies the deployment and management of Ray applications on Kubernetes. It is particularly useful for users looking to run distributed machine learning workloads efficiently. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in managing Ray resources.

steps.title: Add the KubeRay Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeRay Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeRay easily.

```bash

helm repo add kuberay https://ray-project.github.io/kuberay

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official KubeRay Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeRay Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
This step installs the necessary CRDs for KubeRay, which define the custom resources used by KubeRay.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/raycluster.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayjob.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayservice.yaml

```

> Installs the RayCluster CRD.
> Installs the RayJob CRD.
> Installs the RayService CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeRay using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs KubeRay into your Kubernetes cluster using Helm, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm install kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay --create-namespace

```

> Installs KubeRay in the 'kuberay' namespace.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if KubeRay is installed correctly by verifying the status of the KubeRay pods.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

kubectl get crd

```

> Lists all pods in the 'kuberay' namespace to ensure they are running.
> Verifies that the KubeRay CRDs are installed.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeRay
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeRay from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all KubeRay resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kuberay --namespace kuberay

kubectl delete namespace kuberay

```

> Uninstalls KubeRay from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeRay
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade KubeRay to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n 1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> Drains all pods in the KubeRay namespace.
> Upgrades KubeRay to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kuberay -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n 1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error from server (NotFound): customresourcedefinitions.apiextensions.k8s.io "rayclusters.kuberay.io" not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeRay CRDs were not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD installation commands.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install KubeRay: release kuberay failed: timed out waiting for the condition'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeRay pods are not starting due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure sufficient resources are available in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'kuberay' namespace are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the configuration or the underlying Ray application.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kuberay' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in the system PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl and ensure it is in your system PATH.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Ray features and improved stability.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeRay pods running in the 'kuberay' namespace with a status of 'Running'. Use 'kubectl get pods -n kuberay' to verify.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources and the necessary tools installed.
